content.body:
## Problem Statement

Users cannot access the HealthChat AI platform. There is no authentication system in place, meaning anyone could access any data, and there is no way to identify individual users. We need signup, login, session management, and profile management to identify users and properly scope data access with tenant isolation.

## Scope

- Email + password signup/login via Supabase Auth
- JWT session management
- User profile CRUD (display name, DOB)
- Session persistence and refresh token handling

## Child Tickets

_Feature tickets will be linked below as they are created._

## Priority: P0
## Effort: L (2-5d aggregate)
content.title: Epic: User Authentication & Profile

content.body:
## Problem Statement

Users have no way to get their health data into the system. Without file upload and document parsing capabilities, the platform cannot ingest medical reports, lab results, or other health documents. We need file upload to Supabase Storage and AI-powered document parsing via Claude Vision to extract biomarkers and lab values into structured data.

## Scope

- PDF and image file upload to Supabase Storage
- Report record creation in database
- Claude Vision integration for document parsing
- Biomarker and lab value extraction into ParsedResult records

## Child Tickets

_Feature tickets will be linked below as they are created._

## Priority: P0
## Effort: XL (5+d aggregate)
content.title: Epic: Medical Report Upload & Parsing

content.body:
## Problem Statement

Users cannot interact with their health data in a natural way. Raw lab values and medical terminology are meaningless to most users. We need a natural language chat interface powered by Claude AI that lets users ask questions about their health data and receive plain-language explanations with appropriate medical disclaimers.

## Scope

- Chat API endpoint with Claude integration
- Context injection from parsed report data
- WhatsApp-style chat UI with message history
- Typing indicators and streaming responses
- Medical disclaimer on all AI responses

## Child Tickets

_Feature tickets will be linked below as they are created._

## Priority: P0
## Effort: L (2-5d aggregate)
content.title: Epic: Conversational AI Chat

content.body:
## Problem Statement

Users see raw medical data they cannot understand. Lab values like "HDL: 42 mg/dL" or "TSH: 6.2 mIU/L" are meaningless without medical knowledge. We need a simplification engine that translates results to a 5th-grade reading level and a risk flagging system (Green/Yellow/Red) so users can instantly see which values need attention.

## Scope

- Health simplification engine using Claude with grade-level prompts
- Structured summary generation from ParsedResult data
- Risk flag calculation (G/Y/R) from biomarker values vs reference ranges
- Visual risk indicators in the UI

## Child Tickets

_Feature tickets will be linked below as they are created._

## Priority: P1
## Effort: L (2-5d aggregate)
content.title: Epic: Health Simplification & Risk Flagging

content.body:
## Problem Statement

Users don't know what to ask their doctor during appointments. After receiving lab results, most people lack the medical knowledge to formulate meaningful questions about abnormal values or trends. We need auto-generated, personalized doctor visit prep questions based on parsed lab results so users can have more productive medical consultations.

## Scope

- Doctor question generation API using Claude + lab data
- Personalized questions based on flagged biomarkers
- Doctor prep UI for viewing, saving, and printing questions
- Export functionality for appointment preparation

## Child Tickets

_Feature tickets will be linked below as they are created._

## Priority: P1
## Effort: M (0.5-2d aggregate)
content.title: Epic: Doctor Question Generator

content.body:
## Problem Statement

The platform handles Protected Health Information (PHI) but has no compliance layer. Without proper encryption, audit logging, row-level security policies, and security hardening, the platform cannot legally or ethically handle health data. We need a comprehensive HIPAA compliance infrastructure to protect user data and meet regulatory requirements.

## Scope

- Supabase RLS policies on all tables for tenant isolation
- Audit logging for all PHI access events
- Database schema with proper security constraints
- Encryption at rest and in transit verification
- No PHI in application logs

## Child Tickets

_Feature tickets will be linked below as they are created._

## Priority: P0
## Effort: XL (5+d aggregate)
content.title: Epic: HIPAA Compliance & Security

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
New users cannot create accounts or log in. We need Supabase Auth integration with email/password signup, login, and JWT session management to gate access to the platform.

**Parent Epic:** #15 (User Authentication & Profile)
**Effort Estimate:** M
**Priority:** P0

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript (see docs/TECHNICAL-ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Integration points: Supabase Auth, Supabase client library (`@supabase/supabase-js`)
- Existing pattern: Next.js App Router with Route Handlers
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `lib/supabase/client.ts` (browser client)
    - CREATE `lib/supabase/server.ts` (server client)
    - CREATE `app/auth/signup/page.tsx`
    - CREATE `app/auth/login/page.tsx`
    - CREATE `app/api/auth/callback/route.ts`
    - CREATE `middleware.ts` (auth middleware)

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Do NOT store passwords in the application database — Supabase Auth handles this.
- Do NOT log user credentials or tokens.
- Do NOT disable HTTPS or TLS requirements.
- Use environment variables for Supabase URL and anon key — never hardcode.
- Do NOT hardcode redirect URLs — use `window.location.origin`.

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. User navigates to `/auth/signup` and enters email + password.
2. Client calls `supabase.auth.signUp({ email, password })`.
3. Supabase creates user, returns session JWT.
4. Middleware reads JWT from cookie, allows access to protected routes.
5. User navigates to `/auth/login`, enters credentials.
6. Client calls `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({ email, password })`.
7. Supabase validates credentials, returns session JWT.
8. User is redirected to dashboard.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Test asserts signup with valid email/password creates a user session.
- Test asserts login with valid credentials returns a session.
- Test asserts unauthenticated requests to protected routes redirect to `/auth/login`.
- `npm run lint` returns zero errors.
- `npm run typecheck` returns zero errors.
- `npm test` passes all tests.
- PR reviewer can manually sign up, log in, and verify session persistence.
content.title: Supabase Auth integration (signup/login)

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
The reports list sits at the bottom of the dashboard as a long list, making it feel like an afterthought. It should be a dedicated card section alongside Upload, Chat, and Profile — giving the dashboard 4 equal main sections with clear visual hierarchy.

**User feedback:**
- "I want the results to be in its own box so we have 4 main sections instead of long list at the bottom"

**Parent Epic:** Cross-cutting (UI/UX)
**Effort Estimate:** S
**Priority:** P1

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / Pure CSS (BEM)
- Current: Dashboard has 3 cards (Upload, Chat, Profile) in a grid, then ReportList below as a separate section
- File: `app/dashboard/page.tsx`, `components/reports/ReportList.tsx`, `app/globals.css`

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Keep all existing functionality
- Mobile-responsive
- Maintain green health theme from #80

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. Restructure dashboard to 4-card grid: Upload, Chat, Reports, Profile
2. Reports card shows count of reports + latest 2-3 with status badges
3. "View all reports" link leads to a full reports page
4. Create `/reports` page listing all reports (currently only accessible via dashboard)
5. Each report card is clickable to its detail page

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Dashboard shows 4 equal cards in grid
- Reports card shows recent reports with status
- Full reports page exists at /reports
- Mobile-responsive
- All tests pass
content.title: Dashboard: Reports section as a dedicated card with improved layout

items.title: Dashboard: Reports section as a dedicated card with improved layout

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
The app currently has no profile picture support. A user avatar makes the UI feel more personal and modern — it can appear in the dashboard header, chat messages, and profile page.

**Parent Epic:** #15 (User Authentication & Profile)
**Effort Estimate:** M
**Priority:** P1

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript / Supabase Storage
- Existing: `app/profile/page.tsx` has display name, DOB, gender fields
- Existing: `app/api/profile/route.ts` handles GET/PUT for profile data
- Storage: Supabase Storage already configured for file uploads (used by report upload)
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `app/api/profile/avatar/route.ts` — POST (upload), DELETE (remove) avatar
    - MODIFY `app/profile/page.tsx` — add avatar upload section with preview
    - MODIFY `app/dashboard/page.tsx` — show avatar in header
    - MODIFY `components/chat/MessageBubble.tsx` — show avatar on user messages
    - CREATE `components/ui/Avatar.tsx` — reusable avatar component with fallback initials

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Accept only image files (PNG, JPG, JPEG, WebP) — max 2MB
- Store in Supabase Storage bucket (e.g., `avatars/{user_id}`)
- Serve via Supabase Storage public URL or signed URL
- Show initials fallback when no avatar is uploaded
- Round/circular crop display
- Do NOT hardcode any URLs

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. User navigates to `/profile`
2. Profile page shows circular avatar placeholder (initials from display name)
3. User clicks avatar or "Upload Photo" button
4. File picker opens — user selects an image
5. Image is uploaded to Supabase Storage via `POST /api/profile/avatar`
6. Avatar URL is stored in `profiles.avatar_url` column
7. Avatar appears immediately on profile page, dashboard header, and chat bubbles
8. User can click "Remove" to delete the avatar and revert to initials

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Avatar upload endpoint works with auth and file validation
- Profile page shows avatar with upload/remove functionality
- Avatar component renders with fallback initials
- Dashboard header shows user avatar
- Test asserts avatar upload accepts valid images and rejects invalid files
- Test asserts Avatar component renders initials fallback
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors
- `npm test` passes all tests
- PR reviewed and merged
content.title: Profile picture upload — user avatar for modern UI

items.title: Profile picture upload — user avatar for modern UI

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
Authenticated users have no profile data. We need a profile page where users can set their display name and date of birth, which will be used to personalize health explanations and risk assessments.

**Parent Epic:** #15 (User Authentication & Profile)
**Effort Estimate:** S
**Priority:** P1

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript (see docs/TECHNICAL-ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Integration points: Supabase PostgreSQL (profiles table), Supabase Auth (auth.uid())
- Existing pattern: Supabase client from `lib/supabase/client.ts`
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `app/profile/page.tsx`
    - CREATE `app/api/profile/route.ts`

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Do NOT store date of birth in plaintext if possible — use Supabase Vault for encryption.
- Do NOT expose other users' profile data — RLS policy must scope to `auth.uid()`.
- Do NOT allow profile updates without a valid session.
- Do NOT log PHI (date of birth is PHI under HIPAA).

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. Authenticated user navigates to `/profile`.
2. Page loads existing profile data from `GET /api/profile`.
3. User fills in display name and date of birth.
4. Client submits `PUT /api/profile` with `{ display_name, date_of_birth }`.
5. Server validates input, upserts into `profiles` table scoped to `auth.uid()`.
6. Server returns 200 with updated profile.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Test asserts GET /api/profile returns 200 with profile data for authenticated user.
- Test asserts PUT /api/profile updates display_name and date_of_birth.
- Test asserts unauthenticated requests return 401.
- RLS policy verified: user A cannot read user B's profile.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
- `npm test` passes all tests.
content.title: User profile management

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
Users have no way to upload their medical reports. We need a file upload endpoint that accepts PDFs and images, stores them securely in Supabase Storage, and creates a Report record in the database for tracking.

**Parent Epic:** #16 (Medical Report Upload & Parsing)
**Effort Estimate:** M
**Priority:** P0

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript (see docs/TECHNICAL-ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Integration points: Supabase Storage (encrypted bucket), Supabase PostgreSQL (reports table)
- Existing pattern: Next.js Route Handlers with FormData
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `app/api/upload/route.ts`
    - CREATE `app/upload/page.tsx` (upload UI)
    - CREATE `lib/supabase/storage.ts` (storage helpers)

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Do NOT accept files larger than 10MB.
- Do NOT accept file types other than PDF, PNG, JPG, JPEG.
- Do NOT store files without encryption at rest (Supabase Storage handles this).
- Do NOT log file contents or filenames containing PHI.
- Do NOT allow uploads without authentication.
- Use signed URLs for file access — never expose raw storage paths.

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. Authenticated user selects a PDF or image file on the upload page.
2. Client sends POST `/api/upload` with multipart FormData containing the file.
3. Server validates file type and size.
4. Server uploads file to Supabase Storage bucket `medical-reports` with path `{user_id}/{uuid}.{ext}`.
5. Server creates a Report record: `{ user_id, file_path, file_type, original_filename, status: 'uploaded' }`.
6. Server returns 201 with `{ report_id, status: 'uploaded' }`.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Test asserts POST /api/upload with valid PDF returns 201 with report_id.
- Test asserts POST /api/upload with invalid file type returns 400.
- Test asserts POST /api/upload with file > 10MB returns 413.
- Test asserts unauthenticated upload returns 401.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
- `npm test` passes all tests.
- PR reviewer can upload a PDF and verify it appears in Supabase Storage.
content.title: Medical report file upload endpoint

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
Uploaded medical reports sit as raw files with no extracted data. We need to send uploaded documents to Claude Vision for parsing, extracting biomarkers, lab values, units, and reference ranges into a structured ParsedResult record.

**Parent Epic:** #16 (Medical Report Upload & Parsing)
**Effort Estimate:** L
**Priority:** P0

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript (see docs/TECHNICAL-ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Integration points: Anthropic Claude API (`@anthropic-ai/sdk`), Supabase Storage (file retrieval), Supabase PostgreSQL (parsed_results table)
- Existing pattern: Route Handlers in `app/api/`
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `app/api/parse/route.ts`
    - CREATE `lib/claude/client.ts` (Claude API client)
    - CREATE `lib/claude/prompts.ts` (parsing prompt templates)
    - MODIFY Report status to 'parsing' then 'parsed'

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Do NOT send PHI to any AI provider without a BAA in place (Anthropic BAA required).
- Do NOT store raw Claude API responses containing PHI in application logs.
- Do NOT allow parsing without authentication and ownership verification.
- Do NOT modify the original uploaded file.
- Response parsing must handle malformed/unreadable documents gracefully — set status to 'error'.
- Claude prompt MUST instruct the model to extract structured data, NOT provide medical advice.

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. Client or background job sends POST `/api/parse` with `{ report_id }`.
2. Server verifies user owns the report, updates status to 'parsing'.
3. Server retrieves file from Supabase Storage via signed URL.
4. Server sends file (base64-encoded image or PDF) to Claude Vision API with extraction prompt.
5. Claude returns structured JSON: `{ biomarkers: [{ name, value, unit, reference_range, flag }], summary }`.
6. Server validates and stores ParsedResult: `{ report_id, raw_extraction, biomarkers, summary_plain }`.
7. Server updates Report status to 'parsed'.
8. Server returns 200 with `{ parsed_result_id, biomarker_count }`.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Test asserts POST /api/parse with valid report_id returns 200 with parsed data.
- Test asserts parsing updates Report status from 'uploaded' to 'parsing' to 'parsed'.
- Test asserts parsing a non-existent report_id returns 404.
- Test asserts parsing another user's report returns 403.
- Test asserts malformed document sets status to 'error'.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
- `npm test` passes all tests.
- PR reviewer can upload a lab report PDF and see extracted biomarkers.
content.title: Document parsing with Claude Vision

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
Users cannot ask questions about their health data. We need a chat API endpoint that accepts user messages, loads relevant report context, sends it to Claude, and returns plain-language responses at a 5th grade reading level.

**Parent Epic:** #17 (Conversational AI Chat)
**Effort Estimate:** M
**Priority:** P0

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript (see docs/TECHNICAL-ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Integration points: Anthropic Claude API, Supabase PostgreSQL (chat_sessions, chat_messages, parsed_results)
- Existing pattern: Claude client from `lib/claude/client.ts`
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `app/api/chat/route.ts`
    - CREATE `lib/claude/chat-prompts.ts` (system prompts for health chat)
    - CREATE Supabase migration for chat_sessions, chat_messages tables

--- B. Guardrails ---
- ALL responses MUST include disclaimer: "This is not medical advice. Consult your healthcare provider."
- Do NOT provide diagnoses or treatment recommendations.
- Do NOT log chat message content (PHI).
- Do NOT allow chat without authentication.
- Responses MUST be at a 5th grade reading level.
- Do NOT send more than the relevant parsed report data as context (minimize PHI exposure).

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. Authenticated user sends POST `/api/chat` with `{ session_id?, report_id?, message }`.
2. Server creates or loads ChatSession.
3. Server loads ParsedResult for the referenced report (if any).
4. Server constructs Claude prompt: system prompt (5th grade level, no diagnosis, include disclaimer) + report context + chat history + user message.
5. Server calls Claude API with constructed messages.
6. Claude returns plain-language response.
7. Server stores both user message and assistant response in chat_messages table.
8. Server returns 200 with `{ message: { role: 'assistant', content }, session_id }`.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Test asserts POST /api/chat with valid message returns 200 with assistant response.
- Test asserts response contains medical disclaimer text.
- Test asserts chat messages are persisted in database.
- Test asserts unauthenticated requests return 401.
- Test asserts chat with report_id includes report context.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
- `npm test` passes all tests.
- PR reviewer can send a chat message and receive a plain-language response.
content.title: Chat API with Claude

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
There is no user interface for the chat feature. We need a WhatsApp-style chat component with message bubbles, typing indicators, and auto-scroll that connects to the chat API.

**Parent Epic:** #17 (Conversational AI Chat)
**Effort Estimate:** M
**Priority:** P0

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript / Tailwind CSS / shadcn/ui
- Integration points: `/api/chat` endpoint, Supabase Auth (session)
- Existing pattern: shadcn/ui components for base UI elements
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `app/chat/page.tsx`
    - CREATE `components/chat/ChatWindow.tsx`
    - CREATE `components/chat/MessageBubble.tsx`
    - CREATE `components/chat/ChatInput.tsx`
    - CREATE `hooks/useChat.ts` (chat state management)

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Do NOT hardcode API URLs — use relative paths or `window.location.origin`.
- Do NOT render raw HTML from AI responses (XSS risk) — sanitize or use markdown.
- Do NOT persist chat messages in localStorage (PHI).
- Display medical disclaimer prominently in the chat interface.
- Ensure accessibility: keyboard navigation, screen reader support, focus management.

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. User navigates to `/chat` (optionally with `?report_id=xxx`).
2. ChatWindow loads, displays welcome message and disclaimer.
3. User types message in ChatInput and presses send.
4. UI shows user message bubble immediately, shows typing indicator.
5. `useChat` hook sends POST to `/api/chat` with message.
6. On response, typing indicator hides, assistant message bubble appears.
7. Chat auto-scrolls to latest message.
8. Conversation history is maintained in component state for the session.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Component test asserts ChatWindow renders welcome message.
- Component test asserts sending a message shows user bubble and triggers API call.
- Component test asserts assistant response renders in message bubble.
- Component test asserts medical disclaimer is visible.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
- `npm test` passes all tests.
- PR reviewer can open `/chat`, send messages, and see responses rendered correctly.
content.title: Chat UI component

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
Parsed biomarker data is stored as raw values and clinical terms that users cannot understand. We need a simplification engine that processes ParsedResult through Claude with a specialized prompt to generate structured summaries at a 5th grade reading level.

**Parent Epic:** #18 (Health Simplification & Risk Flagging)
**Effort Estimate:** M
**Priority:** P1

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript (see docs/TECHNICAL-ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Integration points: Anthropic Claude API, Supabase PostgreSQL (parsed_results table)
- Existing pattern: Claude client from `lib/claude/client.ts`, parsing prompts from `lib/claude/prompts.ts`
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `app/api/reports/[id]/summary/route.ts`
    - CREATE `lib/claude/simplification-prompts.ts`
    - CREATE `components/reports/HealthSummary.tsx`

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Output MUST be at a 5th grade reading level (Flesch-Kincaid).
- Do NOT provide diagnoses — only explain what values mean.
- Each biomarker explanation MUST follow the structure: what it means, why it matters, what to do next.
- Do NOT log simplified summaries (may contain PHI context).
- Include medical disclaimer in every summary.

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. After report parsing completes, client requests GET `/api/reports/[id]/summary`.
2. Server loads ParsedResult for the report.
3. If `summary_plain` already exists, return cached version.
4. Otherwise, server sends biomarker data to Claude with simplification prompt.
5. Claude returns structured summary: `{ overall: string, biomarkers: [{ name, explanation, importance, action }] }`.
6. Server stores summary in `parsed_results.summary_plain`.
7. Server returns 200 with simplified summary.
8. HealthSummary component renders the summary with clear headings and sections.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Test asserts GET /api/reports/[id]/summary returns structured summary.
- Test asserts summary is cached on subsequent requests.
- Test asserts unauthenticated requests return 401.
- Test asserts accessing another user's report summary returns 403.
- Component test asserts HealthSummary renders biomarker explanations.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
- `npm test` passes all tests.
content.title: Health simplification engine

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
Users see raw biomarker values but have no visual way to understand if their results are normal, borderline, or concerning. We need a risk flagging system that calculates Green/Yellow/Red indicators based on lab values vs reference ranges.

**Parent Epic:** #18 (Health Simplification & Risk Flagging)
**Effort Estimate:** M
**Priority:** P1

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript / Tailwind CSS (see docs/TECHNICAL-ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Integration points: Supabase PostgreSQL (risk_flags, parsed_results tables)
- Existing pattern: ParsedResult biomarker data includes reference ranges
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `app/api/risk-flags/route.ts`
    - CREATE `lib/risk/calculator.ts` (flag calculation logic)
    - CREATE `components/reports/RiskIndicator.tsx`
    - CREATE `components/reports/RiskDashboard.tsx`

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Do NOT present risk flags as medical diagnoses — they are informational indicators only.
- Flag logic must be deterministic (no AI randomness) — based purely on value vs range.
- Green: within reference range. Yellow: within 10% outside range. Red: >10% outside range.
- Do NOT modify parsed biomarker data — risk_flags is a separate table.
- Include disclaimer: "These indicators are for informational purposes only."

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. After report parsing, server calculates risk flags for each biomarker.
2. For each biomarker in ParsedResult: compare value to reference_low and reference_high.
3. Calculate flag: green (in range), yellow (within 10% of boundary), red (>10% outside).
4. Store RiskFlag records: `{ parsed_result_id, biomarker_name, value, reference_low, reference_high, flag }`.
5. Client requests GET `/api/risk-flags?report_id=xxx`.
6. Server returns array of risk flags.
7. RiskDashboard displays color-coded indicators per biomarker.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Test asserts value within range returns green flag.
- Test asserts value within 10% of boundary returns yellow flag.
- Test asserts value >10% outside range returns red flag.
- Test asserts GET /api/risk-flags returns flags for authenticated user's report.
- Component test asserts RiskIndicator renders correct color.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
- `npm test` passes all tests.
content.title: Risk flagging system (Green/Yellow/Red)

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
Users don't know what questions to ask their doctor about their lab results. We need an API endpoint that uses parsed lab data and Claude to auto-generate personalized, categorized doctor visit prep questions.

**Parent Epic:** #19 (Doctor Question Generator)
**Effort Estimate:** M
**Priority:** P1

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript (see docs/TECHNICAL-ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Integration points: Anthropic Claude API, Supabase PostgreSQL (doctor_questions, parsed_results tables)
- Existing pattern: Claude client from `lib/claude/client.ts`
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `app/api/doctor-questions/route.ts`
    - CREATE `lib/claude/doctor-prompts.ts`

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Questions MUST be phrased for the patient to ask their doctor — NOT as medical advice from the AI.
- Do NOT generate questions that assume a diagnosis.
- Do NOT log generated questions (may reference PHI context).
- Include disclaimer: "These questions are suggestions to help guide your conversation with your doctor."
- Limit to 10 questions per report to avoid overwhelming the user.

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. Client sends POST `/api/doctor-questions` with `{ parsed_result_id }`.
2. Server loads ParsedResult and associated risk flags.
3. Server sends biomarker data + risk flags to Claude with doctor-question prompt.
4. Claude returns structured JSON: `[{ question, category, priority }]`.
5. Server stores DoctorQuestion records: `{ parsed_result_id, question, category, priority }`.
6. Server returns 200 with array of generated questions.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Test asserts POST /api/doctor-questions returns array of questions.
- Test asserts each question has category (clarifying|follow_up|lifestyle|medication) and priority.
- Test asserts unauthenticated requests return 401.
- Test asserts accessing another user's report returns 403.
- Test asserts maximum 10 questions per request.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
- `npm test` passes all tests.
content.title: Doctor question generation API

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
Generated doctor questions exist only as API data with no user-facing interface. We need a Doctor Prep page that displays categorized questions with the ability to save or print them for a doctor visit.

**Parent Epic:** #19 (Doctor Question Generator)
**Effort Estimate:** S
**Priority:** P1

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript / Tailwind CSS / shadcn/ui
- Integration points: `/api/doctor-questions` endpoint, Supabase Auth
- Existing pattern: shadcn/ui Card and Button components
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `app/reports/[id]/doctor-prep/page.tsx`
    - CREATE `components/doctor/QuestionList.tsx`
    - CREATE `components/doctor/QuestionCard.tsx`

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Display disclaimer prominently: "These questions are suggestions to help guide your conversation with your doctor."
- Do NOT allow sharing questions via URL (contains PHI context).
- Print view must be clean and professional — no app chrome.
- Do NOT hardcode any URLs.

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. User navigates to `/reports/[id]/doctor-prep`.
2. Page calls GET `/api/doctor-questions?parsed_result_id=xxx` (or triggers generation if none exist).
3. QuestionList renders questions grouped by category (clarifying, follow-up, lifestyle, medication).
4. Each QuestionCard shows the question, category badge, and priority indicator.
5. User clicks "Print" — browser print dialog opens with clean print-friendly layout.
6. User clicks "Save" — questions are bookmarked in their session for later access.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Component test asserts QuestionList renders grouped questions.
- Component test asserts QuestionCard displays question text, category, and priority.
- Component test asserts disclaimer is visible.
- Test asserts print button triggers `window.print()`.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
- `npm test` passes all tests.
- PR reviewer can view the doctor prep page with categorized questions.
content.title: Doctor prep UI

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
No database tables exist for the application. We need to create the complete Supabase schema including profiles, reports, parsed_results, risk_flags, chat_sessions, chat_messages, and doctor_questions tables with proper types, constraints, and indexes.

**Parent Epic:** #20 (HIPAA Compliance & Security)
**Effort Estimate:** M
**Priority:** P0

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / TypeScript, Supabase PostgreSQL (see docs/TECHNICAL-ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Integration points: Supabase Dashboard or CLI for migrations
- Existing pattern: Supabase migration files in `supabase/migrations/`
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `supabase/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql`
    - CREATE `supabase/config.toml` (if not exists)

--- B. Guardrails ---
- All tables MUST have `id` as UUID primary key (use `gen_random_uuid()`).
- All tables MUST have `created_at` timestamp with default `now()`.
- Foreign keys MUST have ON DELETE CASCADE or ON DELETE SET NULL as appropriate.
- Do NOT create RLS policies in this ticket (separate ticket #31).
- Use `jsonb` for flexible fields (biomarkers, raw_extraction).
- Index all `user_id` and `report_id` foreign key columns.

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. Create Supabase migration file with all table definitions.
2. Tables: profiles, reports, parsed_results, risk_flags, chat_sessions, chat_messages, doctor_questions.
3. Each table has proper column types, constraints, and defaults per docs/TECHNICAL-ARCHITECTURE.md data models.
4. Create indexes on frequently queried columns (user_id, report_id, session_id, parsed_result_id).
5. Run migration: `supabase db push` or apply via Supabase Dashboard.
6. Verify all tables created with correct schema.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Migration file creates all 7 tables without errors.
- All foreign key relationships are correct.
- All enum types (report status, flag color, message role, question category) are created.
- Indexes exist on user_id, report_id, session_id, parsed_result_id columns.
- `supabase db push` (or equivalent) runs successfully.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
- PR reviewer can verify table structure matches TECHNICAL-ARCHITECTURE.md data models.
content.title: Database schema setup (all core tables)

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
Database tables have no row-level security. Any authenticated user could potentially access another user's PHI. We need RLS policies on all tables to enforce tenant isolation — users can only read/write their own data.

**Parent Epic:** #20 (HIPAA Compliance & Security)
**Effort Estimate:** M
**Priority:** P0

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Supabase PostgreSQL with RLS (see docs/TECHNICAL-ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Integration points: All application tables, Supabase Auth (`auth.uid()`)
- Existing pattern: Supabase RLS documentation
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `supabase/migrations/002_rls_policies.sql`

--- B. Guardrails ---
- ALL tables containing user data MUST have RLS enabled.
- Policies MUST use `auth.uid()` to scope access — never trust client-provided user_id.
- Do NOT create overly permissive policies (no `USING (true)` on PHI tables).
- Do NOT disable RLS for "convenience" during development.
- Service role key usage must be limited to server-side operations only.

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. Enable RLS on all tables: profiles, reports, parsed_results, risk_flags, chat_sessions, chat_messages, doctor_questions.
2. Create SELECT policy: `USING (user_id = auth.uid())` on each table.
3. Create INSERT policy: `WITH CHECK (user_id = auth.uid())` on each table.
4. Create UPDATE policy: `USING (user_id = auth.uid()) WITH CHECK (user_id = auth.uid())`.
5. Create DELETE policy: `USING (user_id = auth.uid())` on each table.
6. For joined tables (parsed_results, risk_flags, chat_messages, doctor_questions), verify user ownership through parent table join.
7. Run migration and verify policies are active.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Migration enables RLS and creates policies on all 7 tables.
- Test asserts user A cannot SELECT user B's reports.
- Test asserts user A cannot INSERT a report with user B's user_id.
- Test asserts user A cannot UPDATE user B's profile.
- Test asserts user A cannot DELETE user B's chat sessions.
- `supabase db push` runs successfully.
- PR reviewer can verify RLS policies in Supabase Dashboard.
content.title: Supabase RLS policies for tenant isolation

items.title: Supabase RLS policies for tenant isolation

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
There is no record of who accessed what PHI and when. HIPAA requires audit trails for all access to protected health information. We need audit logging that tracks file uploads, report views, chat interactions, and data exports.

**Parent Epic:** #20 (HIPAA Compliance & Security)
**Effort Estimate:** M
**Priority:** P1

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / TypeScript, Supabase PostgreSQL (see docs/TECHNICAL-ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Integration points: All API routes that access PHI, Supabase PostgreSQL
- Existing pattern: Next.js middleware for request interception
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `supabase/migrations/003_audit_log.sql`
    - CREATE `lib/audit/logger.ts`
    - MODIFY API routes to call audit logger

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Audit log entries MUST NOT contain the actual PHI data — only metadata (who, what resource, when, action type).
- Do NOT make audit logging optional or configurable — it must always be on.
- Do NOT allow users to delete or modify audit log entries.
- Audit log table should NOT have RLS user-scoped policies (admin-only access).
- Do NOT block the request if audit logging fails — log the failure and continue.

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. Create `audit_logs` table: `{ id, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, ip_address, timestamp }`.
2. Create `lib/audit/logger.ts` with `logAuditEvent(userId, action, resourceType, resourceId)` function.
3. Add audit logging calls to API routes:
   - `report.upload` when a file is uploaded
   - `report.view` when a report is accessed
   - `report.parse` when parsing is triggered
   - `chat.message` when a chat message is sent
   - `doctor_questions.generate` when questions are generated
4. Audit log writes are fire-and-forget (non-blocking).

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Migration creates audit_logs table with correct schema.
- Test asserts file upload creates an audit log entry.
- Test asserts report view creates an audit log entry.
- Test asserts audit log entries contain user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, timestamp.
- Test asserts audit log entries do NOT contain PHI content.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
- `npm test` passes all tests.
content.title: Audit logging for PHI access

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
Auth pages (login/signup) use raw `<a>` tags for navigation between each other. This causes full page reloads instead of client-side navigation, resulting in a slower user experience.

**Parent Epic:** #15 (User Authentication & Profile)
**Effort Estimate:** S
**Priority:** P2
**Source:** PR #37 review suggestion

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript
- Files to modify:
    - MODIFY `app/auth/login/page.tsx`
    - MODIFY `app/auth/signup/page.tsx`

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Do NOT change any auth logic or Supabase calls.
- Do NOT add new dependencies.

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. Replace `<a href="/auth/signup">` with `<Link href="/auth/signup">` in login page.
2. Replace `<a href="/auth/login">` with `<Link href="/auth/login">` in signup page.
3. Import `Link` from `next/link` in both files.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Both auth pages use Next.js `<Link>` component.
- Navigation between login and signup is client-side (no full reload).
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
- `npm test` passes all tests.
content.title: Use Next.js Link for client-side navigation on auth pages

items.title: Use Next.js Link for client-side navigation on auth pages

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
Client components (login, signup, upload, logout-button) call `createClient()` in the component body, creating a new Supabase client instance on every render. This is wasteful and could cause subtle bugs with stale references.

**Parent Epic:** #15 (User Authentication & Profile)
**Effort Estimate:** S
**Priority:** P2
**Source:** PR #37 review suggestion

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript
- Files to modify:
    - MODIFY `app/auth/login/page.tsx`
    - MODIFY `app/auth/signup/page.tsx`
    - MODIFY `app/upload/page.tsx`
    - MODIFY `app/dashboard/logout-button.tsx`

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Do NOT change any auth or upload logic.
- Do NOT modify the server-side client — only browser client usage.
- Ensure the client is stable across re-renders.

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. Wrap `createClient()` calls in `useMemo` in each client component.
2. Alternatively, move client creation to a singleton pattern in `lib/supabase/client.ts`.
3. Verify all auth and upload flows still work.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Supabase client is not re-created on every render.
- All existing auth and upload tests pass.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
content.title: Optimize Supabase client instantiation with useMemo

items.title: Optimize Supabase client instantiation with useMemo

content.body:
**Problem Statement:**
Auth endpoints (signup, login) have no rate limiting, making them vulnerable to brute-force attacks and credential stuffing. While Supabase has its own rate limits, adding application-level rate limiting provides defense-in-depth for HIPAA compliance.

**Parent Epic:** #20 (HIPAA Compliance & Security)
**Effort Estimate:** M
**Priority:** P2
**Source:** PR #37 review suggestion

--- A. Environment Context ---
- Stack: Next.js 15 / React 19 / TypeScript
- Integration points: Next.js middleware, potentially an in-memory or Redis-based rate limiter
- Files to create or modify:
    - CREATE `lib/rate-limit.ts` (rate limiting utility)
    - MODIFY `middleware.ts` (apply rate limiting to auth routes)

--- B. Guardrails ---
- Do NOT block legitimate users — use reasonable thresholds (e.g., 5 attempts per minute per IP).
- Do NOT store rate limit data in Supabase — use in-memory or edge-compatible store.
- Do NOT log IP addresses with PHI.
- Return 429 Too Many Requests with a Retry-After header.

--- C. Happy Path ---
1. Create a rate limiting utility using a sliding window algorithm.
2. Apply rate limiting in middleware for `/auth/*` and `/api/auth/*` routes.
3. Track attempts by IP address.
4. Return 429 when threshold is exceeded.
5. Include Retry-After header in 429 responses.

--- D. Definition of Done ---
- Test asserts 429 is returned after exceeding rate limit.
- Test asserts normal requests pass through under the limit.
- Test asserts Retry-After header is present on 429 responses.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` return zero errors.
- `npm test` passes all tests.
content.title: Add rate limiting to auth endpoints

items.title: Add rate limiting to auth endpoints
